problem.symptom: Samsung QA runbook blocked by strict compile dbt0227 on new Samsung columns, intermittent dbt1308 view materialization failure, and missing MetricFlow CLI

triage_path.hypothesis: Deferred/prod refs are masking local schema additions during strict compile
triage_path.check: Run strict compile with and without defer
triage_path.result: Compile fails with dbt0227 under deferred resolution and passes with --no-defer

triage_path.hypothesis: Redshift view materialization instability matches known bind/rename issue
triage_path.check: Apply bind=false to deduped view model and re-run scoped no-defer build
triage_path.query_or_action: edit int_samsung__external_details_deduped.sql config + dbt build --no-defer --select int_samsung__external_details int_samsung__external_details_deduped
triage_path.result: Deduped model build and tests passed; dbt1308 no longer reproduced

triage_path.hypothesis: MetricFlow can be validated via a Python 3.12 tool environment when repo venv uses unsupported Python 3.14
triage_path.check: Install dbt-metricflow with redshift adapter extras via uv and run validation
triage_path.result: MetricFlow validation passed (0 errors, 0 warnings)

resolution.fix: Use no-defer strict compile/build for Samsung scope, set bind=false on int_samsung__external_details_deduped view, and run MetricFlow through uv-installed Python 3.12 dbt-metricflow with redshift extras
resolution.root_cause: Mixed execution contexts (deferred schema resolution + Redshift view materialization behavior + Python/runtime incompatibility for MetricFlow) produced recurring non-logic blockers

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: QA runs where new columns exist in dev schema but deferred/prod refs lag; intermittent redshift view materialization relation-not-found; mf unavailable due Python version constraints
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Stable single-environment runs without defer, with native mf available in project-supported Python version

problem.symptom: Copilot review flagged population penetration references to non-existent summary columns (avg_annual_value, active_rate)

triage_path.hypothesis: Upstream state summary should expose all downstream-derived columns
triage_path.check: Added placeholder avg_annual_value and duplicated active_rate upstream, then compiled/ran impacted models
triage_path.result: Technically valid but introduces non-canonical placeholder field with unclear business semantics

triage_path.hypothesis: Derived fields should be computed in downstream model if base fields are available
triage_path.check: Removed avg_annual_value dependency and derived active_rate in population penetration from active_card_rate
triage_path.result: Compile and run passed; design is cleaner and keeps state_summary canonical

triage_path.hypothesis: End-to-end QA sign-off requires full scoped build + semantic validation
triage_path.check: Ran full demographics+geography dbt build and demographics semantic smoke suite
triage_path.query_or_action: dbt build --select <full scope>; mf validate-configs + mf query
triage_path.result: 59/59 build success and semantic suite passed with total consistency

resolution.fix: Use downstream derivation pattern for review-flagged metrics and complete full pipeline + semantic revalidation
resolution.root_cause: Downstream model referenced fields not present in upstream summary and implied ownership of non-canonical derived metric

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: When PR review flags missing upstream columns that can be derived from existing downstream inputs
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: When the metric is canonical and intentionally reused across multiple downstream marts

problem.symptom: Demographics QA revalidation blocked by mart contract type mismatch and DOB quality gate below threshold

triage_path.hypothesis: Contract YAML data types diverged from compiled SQL output after DOB decrypt refactor
triage_path.check: Run dbt compile/run on mart_demo__account_demographics_base and inspect contract enforcement error
triage_path.result: Compilation/runtime error listed mismatches on acct_uid and display/day fields (declared varchar/smallint/integer vs compiled bigint)

triage_path.hypothesis: Updating contract types to compiled output will unblock materialization without changing model logic
triage_path.check: Patch _mart_demo__models.yml types and rerun compile/run/test
triage_path.result: Mart build and tests passed after type alignment

triage_path.hypothesis: DOB quality threshold failure is caused by overly narrow decrypt window, not join breakage
triage_path.check: Compare dob_quality_flag distribution with default window, then rerun with dob_decrypt_years=3 and rebuild downstream mart
triage_path.result: valid DOB improved from 72.15% to 94.10%; row parity and funnel monotonicity remained intact

resolution.fix: Align mart contract data types to compiled output and widen DOB decrypt window for QA revalidation
resolution.root_cause: Schema contract drift plus insufficient DOB decrypt window for expected quality threshold

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: When contract-enforced marts fail after upstream type changes and DOB-quality gates regress after decrypt-window changes
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: When business intent requires strict fixed contract types regardless of compiled expression output

problem.symptom: Pivot consistency QA reported ~99% mismatches between cohort_progress and cohort_metrics

triage_path.hypothesis: Materialization/performance changes introduced data drift
triage_path.check: Run legacy pivot-consistency query comparing cohort_progress vs cohort_metrics
triage_path.result: High mismatch counts observed (passed=26870, failed=26919 over 26962 rows)

triage_path.hypothesis: Mismatch is caused by NULL comparison semantics, not value drift
triage_path.check: Re-run comparison with coalesce(...,0) for passed/failed cumulative fields
triage_path.result: Mismatches dropped to zero; null-heavy rows in source aggregate explained false positives

resolution.fix: Use NULL-safe equality (coalesce) in pivot consistency QA checks for sparse cohort metrics
resolution.root_cause: Legacy QA query used direct equality where both sides were frequently NULL, producing false mismatches

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Sparse EAV-to-pivot cohort checks where a metric may be absent on many dates
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Checks where NULL has explicit business meaning distinct from zero

problem.symptom: CI unique test failures (66M duplicates) in downstream models

triage_path.hypothesis: Fan-out in FPA enrichment join
triage_path.check: Code review of int_interchange__account_product_enriched.sql
triage_path.result: Found non-deduplicated join to ODS sources (product_channel, fpa_group)

resolution.fix: Refactored join to use row_number() deduplication logic
resolution.root_cause: ODS sources returning multiple rows preventing 1:1 account enrichment

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: When joining ODS sources for enrichment where 1:1 is assumed
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: When history preservation is required

problem.symptom: 3,761 failures dropped (0.27%) due to NULL failure_reason

triage_path.hypothesis: Filters excluding valid rows
triage_path.check: Checked WHERE clauses
triage_path.result: Found `where failure_reason is not null` but data had NULLs

resolution.fix: Added coalesce(failure_reason, 'Unknown') and removed restrictive filter
resolution.root_cause: Implicit drop of NULL values in failure_reason column

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: When counting failures/status where the field might be NULL for some valid cases
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: When NULL strictly means invalid data

problem.symptom: Incremental run time degraded (28 min / fail) after backfill

triage_path.hypothesis: CTEs doing full table scans on late arriving data joins
triage_path.check: Add date filters to CTEs used in left joins
triage_path.result: Runtime improved from 28m to 2.6m

resolution.fix: Added date filters to gbos_auth and ewallet CTEs
resolution.root_cause: Missing predicate pushdown on incremental runs causing full scan of referenced CTEs

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Incremental models taking >10m where CTEs are large
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Small dimension tables

problem.symptom: Test failure: 'accepted_values' for ProgramCode found 'tpg' (expected only 'dayforce')

triage_path.hypothesis: INNER JOIN to profile_bank_accounts implicitly filters for Dayforce
triage_path.check: Remove explicit WHERE clause and run tests
triage_path.result: Failed. 'tpg' records leaked into dataset.

resolution.fix: Restored explicit 'WHERE ProgramCode = dayforce' filter
resolution.root_cause: TPG program transactions share bank profiles with Dayforce, making JOIN insufficient for filtering

triage_path.result: Table dropped successfully

triage_path.hypothesis: Incremental merge strategy failing due to duplicates in source

triage_path.result: Found exact match in troubleshooting.md with solution

resolution.fix: Changed incremental_strategy from 'merge' to 'delete+insert'
resolution.root_cause: Source query produces duplicate rows for unique_key 'auth_uid' (likely from txn_type_hierarchy join), causing merge failure

problem.symptom: MetricFlow queries for approval-rate metrics returned 0 (unexpected) despite non-zero numerator/denominator

triage_path.hypothesis: Derived metric math is using integer division
triage_path.check: Run mf query for approval rates and compare to raw numerator/denominator
triage_path.result: Approval rates showed 0 while attempt/approve metrics were non-zero

triage_path.hypothesis: Expression needs float coercion
triage_path.check: Update derived metric expressions to enforce float division
triage_path.query_or_action: Edit metrics YAML and re-run dbt parse + mf query
triage_path.result: Approval rates returned expected decimals (e.g., 0.96 / 0.45)

resolution.fix: Coerced derived divisions to float using 1.0 * numerator / NULLIF(denominator, 0)
resolution.root_cause: Integer division in derived metric expressions truncated ratios to 0

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Derived ratio metrics return 0/1 only in MetricFlow despite reasonable raw counts
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: When ratios are already decimals and issues stem from filters or empty result sets

questions_raised.question: Is it true that: Derived metric math is using integer division?
Is it true that: Expression needs float coercion?

problem.symptom: MetricFlow queries for POS entry mode filtered metrics returned empty results due to filter values not matching the underlying dimension strings

triage_path.hypothesis: The semantic dimension values feeding the metric do not match the filter strings used by metrics/saved queries
triage_path.check: Inspect distinct pos_entry_mode values in the semantic base and compare to expected filter set
triage_path.query_or_action: dbt show --inline "select pos_entry_mode, count(*) from {{ ref('mrt_merchant_auth_decline__semantic_base') }} group by 1 order by 2 desc"
triage_path.result: Observed verbose EDW descriptions (e.g., 'Card Present - Chip') rather than the expected short values (e.g., 'Chip')
triage_path.led_to: Normalize pos_entry_mode via the EDW POS entry mode dimension

triage_path.hypothesis: stg_edw__dim_pos_entry_mode.business_category provides the canonical category mapping needed for metric filters
triage_path.check: Join to stg_edw__dim_pos_entry_mode and map business_category to the standardized filter strings
triage_path.query_or_action: Update semantic base SQL to join dim and CASE-map business_category to 'Chip'/'Contactless'/'Magnetic Stripe'/'Manual Entry'
triage_path.result: Rebuilt semantic bases and MetricFlow queries for POS modes returned non-empty results

resolution.fix: Normalized pos_entry_mode outputs in merchant auth decline semantic base models by joining stg_edw__dim_pos_entry_mode and mapping business_category to expected filter strings
resolution.root_cause: Metrics/saved queries filtered on standardized POS entry mode labels while the raw fact table exposed verbose EDW descriptions

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: MetricFlow returns empty results only when applying categorical filters; distinct raw values look similar-but-not-equal to filter strings
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Filters match raw dimension values exactly but results are still empty (likely date scoping or join exclusions)

questions_raised.question: Is it true that: The semantic dimension values feeding the metric do not match the filter strings used by metrics/saved queries?
Is it true that: stg_edw__dim_pos_entry_mode.business_category provides the canonical category mapping needed for metric filters?

problem.symptom: dbt-fusion CI-like preflight failed because YAML selector definitions with embedded excludes were rejected; CI predictor needed a fusion-compatible selection+exclude approach

triage_path.hypothesis: dbt-fusion is stricter than dbt-core and does not accept YAML selector definitions that include YAML-level exclude blocks
triage_path.check: Run dbt-fusion parse/compile using selector YAML that included excludes
triage_path.query_or_action: dbt-fusion parse/compile with selectors.yml selector containing YAML-level exclude blocks
triage_path.result: fusion rejected the selector YAML format and failed before compile could be used as a CI predictor
triage_path.led_to: Move excludes out of YAML and into CLI flags

triage_path.hypothesis: The defer/state artifact path expected by the runbook differs from actual local artifacts
triage_path.check: Locate dbt Cloud defer artifacts locally
triage_path.result: Defer artifacts were present under target/dbt_cloud_defer (not ./target-defer/)
triage_path.led_to: Use correct state/defer path in preflight script

triage_path.hypothesis: A simple include-only selector + CLI excludes will be accepted by fusion and preserve ownership boundaries
triage_path.check: Simplify selectors.yml to include-only and apply non-owned exclusions via --exclude in a script
triage_path.query_or_action: Update selectors.yml to include-only selector (state:modified+1) and enforce excludes via scripts/fusion_preflight.sh
triage_path.result: dbt-fusion parse + compile succeeded with ownership exclusions enforced via CLI

resolution.fix: Made the CI selector fusion-compatible by removing YAML-level excludes (include-only selector) and enforcing non-owned excludes via CLI flags in fusion_preflight.sh, using target/dbt_cloud_defer for state/defer
resolution.root_cause: dbt-fusion rejects certain selector YAML constructs that dbt-core tolerates (notably embedded exclude blocks), so the preflight needed to express exclusions at the CLI layer

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: fusion fails early on selectors.yml parsing or reports unsupported selector YAML keys; you need CI-like selection plus strict ownership excludes
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: selectors work in fusion but the run still fails later (model compilation/runtime issues rather than selector parsing)

questions_raised.question: Is it true that: dbt-fusion is stricter than dbt-core and does not accept YAML selector definitions that include YAML-level exclude blocks?
Is it true that: The defer/state artifact path expected by the runbook differs from actual local artifacts?
Is it true that: A simple include-only selector + CLI excludes will be accepted by fusion and preserve ownership boundaries?

errors_encountered.error_message: dbt-fusion CI-like preflight failed because YAML selector definitions with embedded excludes were rejected; CI predictor needed a fusion-compatible selection+exclude approach

problem.symptom: CI failures: 'ERROR creating sql view_create_or_replace model' - materialization macro not found

triage_path.hypothesis: Models use non-standard materialization that doesn't exist
triage_path.check: Search for macro definition
triage_path.result: Macro file doesn't exist. 3 models reference it.

triage_path.hypothesis: Macro was deleted in recent commit
triage_path.check: Review recent commits affecting macros
triage_path.result: Macro deleted in revert commit 6890751 as 'experimental macro'
triage_path.led_to: Try changing models to standard view

triage_path.hypothesis: Can use standard 'view' materialization instead
triage_path.check: Change materialized='view_create_or_replace' to materialized='view' and compile
triage_path.query_or_action: multi_replace_string_in_file + dbt compile --select int_dates int_oct__aggregated_transfers qa_oct__deduplication_impact_summary
triage_path.result: OCT models failed with 'upstream_prod ref resolution' errors. int_dates compiled.

triage_path.hypothesis: Restoring simple macro is cleaner than debugging ref issues
triage_path.check: Recreate view_create_or_replace.sql and revert models
triage_path.query_or_action: create_file macros/materializations/view_create_or_replace.sql + revert 3 models back to view_create_or_replace
triage_path.result: Local compile still failed for OCT models with upstream_prod errors

triage_path.hypothesis: Stale target/ cache causing upstream_prod resolution issues
triage_path.check: Clear cache and reinstall dependencies
triage_path.query_or_action: dbt clean && dbt deps && dbt compile --select int_oct__aggregated_transfers qa_oct__deduplication_impact_summary
triage_path.result: All models compiled successfully. Pre-push tests passed (61 tests, 59 success, 2 warn).

resolution.fix: Restored macros/materializations/view_create_or_replace.sql (14-line CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW wrapper) and reverted 3 models back to using it
resolution.root_cause: Revert commit deleted macro but left model references intact. Local compilation initially succeeded due to cached target/ but CI failed in clean environment.

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: CI fails with 'materialization not found' but local compile succeeds - indicates stale cache masking deleted dependency
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Materialization truly doesn't exist and never did - should use standard materializations

questions_raised.question: Is it true that: Models use non-standard materialization that doesn't exist?
Is it true that: Macro was deleted in recent commit?

Is it true that: Restoring simple macro is cleaner than debugging ref issues?

problem.symptom: Merge job failures persisted after introducing optimized cohort models because downstream semantic model still referenced old mart; old implementation timed out due to cohort×date cross-join fanout

triage_path.hypothesis: New optimized models were created but not actually consumed downstream; the merge job still runs the legacy mart that WLM-times out
triage_path.check: Inspect materialization + lineage: confirm whether mrt_gbos_registration_cohort_progress is still a table and whether semantic model refs were updated
triage_path.query_or_action: Review model configs + downstream refs; run dbt compile/build for cohort chain
triage_path.result: Confirmed legacy mart remained in lineage; downstream references were not migrated

resolution.fix: Converted mrt_gbos_registration_cohort_progress to a backward-compatibility VIEW that pivots from mrt_gbos_registration_cohort_metrics (EAV) into the legacy wide schema
resolution.root_cause: Downstream semantic models/views still referenced the legacy mart; replacing logic without migration plan caused the old slow model to keep executing

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Refactoring/replacing a model with many downstream consumers (semantic models, BI views) where updating all refs is risky; use a backward-compat view to preserve contract
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Greenfield models or small ref surface area where updating downstream refs is trivial

questions_raised.question: Is it true that: New optimized models were created but not actually consumed downstream; the merge job still runs the legacy mart that WLM-times out?

errors_encountered.error_message: Merge job failures persisted after introducing optimized cohort models because downstream semantic model still referenced old mart; old implementation timed out due to cohort×date cross-join fanout

problem.symptom: mcc_desc missing in dbt show (upstream prod fallback) + dbt0301 mismatch

triage_path.result: dbt0301 error confirmed

triage_path.hypothesis: Upstream Prod (Deferral) masking local changes in dbt show
triage_path.check: Run with upstream_prod_enabled: false
triage_path.result: mcc_desc populated (99.8%)

resolution.fix: Sync CTE columns in UNION ALL and disable upstream deferral for validation
resolution.root_cause: Shift Left adding columns requires updating all UNION parts + Deferral hides local schema changes

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Adding columns to models involved in UNIONs or when dbt show ignores local changes
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Standard linear models without deferral

problem.symptom: VS Code crash caused loss of chat history and context; needed to recover executed queries from Redshift execution logs before they expired

triage_path.hypothesis: Agent account queries are logged in stl_query/stl_querytext on Redshift
triage_path.check: Identify service account for dbt execution
triage_path.query_or_action: Query pg_user joined with stl_query to find top users
triage_path.result: Identified 'svc_dbt_bi' as the primary executor on Apple cluster

triage_path.hypothesis: Directly querying stl_query via dbt show allows dumping history to local JSON
triage_path.check: Execute dbt show --inline query and capture output
triage_path.result: Recovered ~13k queries for Apple (ws3) and Enterprise (ws3) clusters

triage_path.hypothesis: STL logs rotate quickly (2-7 days); permanent history table needed
triage_path.check: Create dbt macro to materialize cumulative stats
triage_path.result: Successfully created mstr_surf.svcmicrostrategy_compute_stats_v2 and backfilled data

resolution.fix: Recovered query history to JSON session logs and established permanent history table via maintenance macro
resolution.root_cause: Local IDE crash wiped session context; Redshift logs are ephemeral unless materialized

reuse_guidance.when_applicable: Recovering lost agent session context or auditing dbt execution when local logs are unavailable
reuse_guidance.when_not_applicable: Normal development with persistent chat history

problem.symptom: Samsung decline analysis showed 44.7% as generic 'Declined' — unhelpful for diagnosis

investigation.approach: Upstream drill-down from semantic layer to raw ISO 8583 response codes in int_samsung__external_details
key_queries: Grouped network_association_response_code for declined txns where network_status_description = 'Declined'

finding.root_cause: 61% of generic 'Declined' (42,950 txns / $5.4M) were ISO 8583 code 59 (suspected fraud) from neobank issuers. Bancorp Bank (51%) and Stride Bank (16%) flagged Samsung P2P as suspicious because they didn't recognize the origination pattern.
finding.resolution: Code 59 has self-resolved 97.6% (31K → 737/month) as issuers learned the pattern. Residual problem is NSF (37.5%) + Service not allowed (36.4%) requiring partner-side BIN config and balance checks.
finding.counterfactual: Samsung's true decline rate excluding fraud flags is ~32.5%, not the reported 45.9%. Code 59 still adds ~13pp.

decision.action: Updated data story Beat 5 with ISO 8583 breakdown chart + bank concentration analysis. Documented upstream drill-down as a reusable analysis pattern in Analytics Manager napkin.
decision.rationale: Generic categories in semantic layer dimensions hide actionable detail. The upstream drill-down pattern enriches — doesn't replace — certified metrics.
